question: Consider the two-dimensional fully frustrated XY (FFXY) model on a square lattice with nearest-neighbor (NN) coupling J and next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) coupling xJ with x=0.5, at external magnetic flux f=1/2 per elementary plaquette. Spins are classical XY (planar) rotors. The low-temperature phase (x < x_0 = 1/sqrt(2)) has both continuous U(1) quasi-long-range order and discrete Z_2 (staggered plaquette chirality) order, giving rise to two separate phase transitions. Using Monte Carlo simulation on square lattices up to L=72 with Binder-cumulant analysis of the staggered chiral magnetization, determine the Z_2 chiral ordering transition temperature T_c^{Z2} in units of J/k_B (quantity: kBTc_Z2_over_J). Output a JSON object {"kBTc_Z2_over_J": <value>}.

metadata.system: 2D fully frustrated XY model on square lattice, NN coupling J + NNN coupling xJ with x=0.5, flux f=1/2
metadata.source_excerpt: for x=0.5 Monte Carlo simulations with lattice sizes up to L=72 and Binder-cumulant analysis of the staggered chiral magnetization yield the estimate k_B T_c^{Z_2}/J=0.403±0.003
metadata.difficulty_note: Z2 chirality transition in FFXY model; distinct from the BKT U(1) transition; model has two separate transition temperatures

cold_solve.probe_model: claude-sonnet baseline (a cloud HPC platform C-MC, async, 1h watchdog)
